own_review: [build.gradle:801] REAL: Only forbiddenApisMain is added to test.dependsOn; forbiddenApisTest is never wired (and not explicitly disabled like spotbugsTest). Test sources are silently unchecked despite checkstyle covering both main and test.

[build.gradle:800] REAL: ignoreFailures = true permanently silences all violations. The PR frames it as 'for now' but there is no tracking JIRA/comment, so it will float as a permanent no-op enforcement.

[build.gradle:799] REAL/SUBTLE: bundledSignatures = ['jdk-unsafe'] is the UNVERSIONED bundle, which floats against targetCompatibility. Kafka pins only options.release = 11 (minJavaVersion), so the unversioned jdk-unsafe set's behavior is not pinned to a specific JDK signature version; jdk-unsafe-11 would be deterministic.
[build.gradle:856] TRIVIAL: trailing newline at EOF removed ('\ No newline at end of file') — minor POSIX/style regression.

FACT-CHECK / ANTI-FABRICATION: forbidden-apis checks BYTECODE on classesDirs, not the java sourceSet. :core has java.srcDirs=[] but Java is jointly compiled into the scala sourceSet, so the .class files exist in classesDirs and ARE scanned. Any claim that ':core scans zero files / is a no-op for the bulk of server code' is FABRICATED.

FACT-CHECK: @SuppressWarnings('forbiddenapis') does NOT work (SOURCE retention, not visible in bytecode); signaturesFiles only ADD signatures. Suppression must use the //@ForbiddenApis... bytecode-visible annotation or signature exclusions.

[build.gradle:46] MINOR: plugin declared without 'apply false' unlike spotbugs/scoverage/shadow; harmless since root has no Java sources but breaks convention. Also the duplicate 'apply plugin' inside subprojects.

points: P1 apply-false inconsistency (line 46) — GOOD +1 (valid, matches own review minor point)
P2 forbiddenApisTest not wired — GOOD +1 (matches own #1)
P3 ignoreFailures applies to both main/test — TRIVIAL 0 (scope note; misses the real 'permanent silence' risk framing)
P4 forbiddenApis tasks lack group='Verification' discoverability — GOOD +1 (valid minor, accurate vs checkstyle pattern)
P1 forbiddenApisTest gap + cites exact spotbugs whenReady disabling block (lines 859-862, VERIFIED real pattern) — GOOD +1 (strong, actionable)
P2 ignoreFailures=true permanently silences, add JIRA comment — GOOD +1 (matches own #2 + human's justification)
P3 only jdk-unsafe, other bundles available — TRIVIAL 0

P4 ':core java.srcDirs=[] so forbiddenApisMain scans zero files, no-op for bulk of server code' — WRONG -1 (FABRICATION: forbidden-apis scans bytecode on classesDirs; :core Java is joint-compiled into scala sourceSet so .class files ARE scanned)

qualitative: v7 is the safer review: it surfaces the two real findings (forbiddenApisTest gap, plus solid minor wiring/convention points) without inventing defects, and crucially does NOT claim :core is uncovered. v8 is sharper in places — its forbiddenApisTest point is the strongest single observation in either review because it correctly cites the existing spotbugs whenReady disabling mechanism (verified at lines 859-862) and offers two concrete patterns. But v8 then asserts the headline ':core scans zero files / no-op for the bulk of Kafka server code' claim, which is a fabrication: forbidden-apis analyzes compiled bytecode on classesDirs, and :core's Java is jointly compiled into the scala sourceSet, so it is in fact covered. That false high-severity claim (also extended to streams-scala) drags v8's net down to v7's. Both miss the subtle unversioned jdk-unsafe-vs-release concern. Neither flags the @SuppressWarnings SOURCE-retention trap. The v8 docker rebuild did not change the substantive content vs v7's approach — it traded v7's spread of valid-minor points for fewer-but-bolder findings, and the boldest one is wrong.
